title: cordia dentata
intro: Cordia dentata, also known as the Wild Orange or Horse Jaw, is a tropical tree native to Central and South America, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cordia dentata include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems. Cordia dentata is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its herbal preparations are also used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
